docs.title: Rocket 68
docs.text: Rocket 68 is a Motorola 68000 emulator written in C11. It is designed to be embedded in host emulators and tools.

docs.title: What This Project Provides
docs.text:
• A CPU core centered on one struct: M68kCpu
  • Flat-memory emulation with 8/16/32-bit big-endian access helpers
  • Instruction stepping (m68k_step) and cycle-budget execution (m68k_execute)
  • Per-instance callbacks for bus wait states, interrupt acknowledge, function code, and debug hooks
  • Exception handling (including bus/address-error frames)
  • Optional loader utilities (m68k_load_srec, m68k_load_bin)
  • Optional disassembler utility (m68k_disasm)

docs.title: Important Scope Notes
docs.text:
• The public API is in include/m68k.h.
  • Address accesses are masked to 24-bit internally (0x00FFFFFF) before bounds checks.
  • The project currently includes some post-68000 opcodes (MOVEC, MOVES, RTD, and BKPT), but does not expose a full CPU-model selection API yet.

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
• include/: public headers (m68k.h, loader.h, disasm.h, rocket68.h)
  • src/m68k/: CPU core and opcode handlers
  • src/loader.c: S-record and raw binary loaders
  • src/disasm.c: disassembler implementation
  • tests/: unit, regression, and JSON compatibility tests
  • benches/: Rocket68 vs Musashi and Moira benchmarks

docs.title: Documentation Map
docs.text:
• Getting Started: build + first runnable program
  • Examples: focused usage patterns
  • API Reference: function groups and behavior notes
  • Compatibility Notes: current scope limits and integration caveats

docs.title: Version
docs.text: • ROCKET68_VERSION_STR: see include/rocket68.h

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This page documents the public C API in:

  • include/m68k.h
  • include/loader.h
  • include/disasm.h
  • include/rocket68.h

docs.title: Header Usage
docs.text:
Include the umbrella header:

#include "rocket68.h"

Or include only what you need:

#include "m68k.h"
#include "loader.h"
#include "disasm.h"

docs.title: Integer aliases
docs.text:
• u8, u16, u32
  • s8, s16, s32

docs.title: M68kSize
docs.text: typedef enum { SIZE_BYTE = 1, SIZE_WORD = 2, SIZE_LONG = 4 } M68kSize;

docs.title: M68kRegister
docs.text: Register union used for D0-D7 and A0-A7. w maps to the low 16 bits of l.

docs.title: M68kCpu
docs.text: One complete CPU instance with registers, execution state, memory binding, and callbacks. d_regs is aligned to 64 bytes.

docs.title: Status Register Flags
docs.text:
• M68K_SR_C carry
  • M68K_SR_V overflow
  • M68K_SR_Z zero
  • M68K_SR_N negative
  • M68K_SR_X extend
  • M68K_SR_S supervisor mode

docs.title: Function Code Constants
docs.text:
• M68K_FC_USER_DATA
  • M68K_FC_USER_PROG
  • M68K_FC_SUPV_DATA
  • M68K_FC_SUPV_PROG
  • M68K_FC_INT_ACK

docs.title: Interrupt ACK Constants
docs.text:
• M68K_INT_ACK_AUTOVECTOR
  • M68K_INT_ACK_SPURIOUS

docs.title: void m68k_init(M68kCpu* cpu, u8* memory, u32 memory_size);
docs.text: Initializes all CPU fields and binds a flat memory buffer.

docs.title: void m68k_reset(M68kCpu* cpu);
docs.text:
Performs reset-state initialization and loads:

  • initial SSP from 0x00000000
  • initial PC from 0x00000004

docs.title: void m68k_set_pc(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 pc);
docs.text: Sets PC and triggers pc_changed callback if installed.

docs.title: u32 m68k_get_pc(M68kCpu* cpu);
docs.text: Returns PC.

docs.title: void m68k_set_sr(M68kCpu* cpu, u16 new_sr);
docs.text: Sets SR with masking (0xA71F) and performs USP/SSP swap when supervisor state changes.

docs.title: void m68k_set_irq(M68kCpu* cpu, int level);
docs.text: Sets pending interrupt level (0-7).

docs.title: Register accessors
docs.text:
• void m68k_set_dr(M68kCpu* cpu, int reg, u32 value);
  • u32 m68k_get_dr(M68kCpu* cpu, int reg);
  • void m68k_set_ar(M68kCpu* cpu, int reg, u32 value);
  • u32 m68k_get_ar(M68kCpu* cpu, int reg);

Invalid register indexes are ignored on set and return 0 on get.

docs.title: void m68k_step_ex(M68kCpu* cpu, bool check_exceptions);
docs.text: Executes one instruction with optional interrupt/trace checks.

docs.title: void m68k_step(M68kCpu* cpu);
docs.text: Equivalent to m68k_step_ex(cpu, true).

docs.title: int m68k_execute(M68kCpu* cpu, int cycles);
docs.text: Adds cycles to the timeslice, runs until cycles_remaining <= 0, and returns consumed cycles for this call.

docs.title: Timeslice helpers
docs.text:
• int m68k_cycles_run(M68kCpu* cpu);
  • int m68k_cycles_remaining(M68kCpu* cpu);
  • void m68k_modify_timeslice(M68kCpu* cpu, int cycles);
  • void m68k_end_timeslice(M68kCpu* cpu);

docs.title: Memory API
docs.text: All memory accesses are big-endian. Addresses are masked to 24-bit internally before bounds checks.

docs.title: Reads
docs.text:
• u8 m68k_read_8(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address);
  • u16 m68k_read_16(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address);
  • u32 m68k_read_32(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address);

docs.title: Writes
docs.text:
• void m68k_write_8(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address, u8 value);
  • void m68k_write_16(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address, u16 value);
  • void m68k_write_32(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address, u32 value);

Behavior notes:

  • Word/long accesses on odd addresses trigger address error handling.
  • Out-of-range accesses trigger bus error handling.

docs.title: Callback API
docs.text: All callbacks are per-instance.

docs.title: void m68k_set_wait_bus_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kWaitBusCallback callback);
docs.text: Called before each memory/fetch bus access.

docs.title: void m68k_set_int_ack_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kIntAckCallback callback);
docs.text: Called when an interrupt is acknowledged. Return vector number, M68K_INT_ACK_AUTOVECTOR, or M68K_INT_ACK_SPURIOUS.

docs.title: void m68k_set_fc_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kFcCallback callback);
docs.text: Called before bus/program accesses with the active FC value.

docs.title: void m68k_set_instr_hook_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kInstrHookCallback callback);
docs.text: Called before each instruction decode/execute (not during STOP-idle cycles).

docs.title: void m68k_set_pc_changed_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kPcChangedCallback callback);
docs.text: Called when PC changes through m68k_set_pc.

docs.title: void m68k_set_reset_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kResetCallback callback);
docs.text: Called by execution of the RESET instruction. This is separate from m68k_reset().

docs.title: void m68k_set_tas_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kTasCallback callback);
docs.text: Called by TAS; non-zero return allows write-back, zero blocks write-back.

docs.title: void m68k_set_illg_callback(M68kCpu* cpu, M68kIllgCallback callback);
docs.text: Registers an illegal-opcode callback pointer. Current core decode path does not invoke this callback yet.

docs.title: unsigned int m68k_context_size(void);
docs.text: Returns context blob size in bytes.

docs.title: void m68k_get_context(M68kCpu* cpu, void* dst);
docs.text: Copies CPU context into dst (m68k_context_size() bytes).

docs.title: void m68k_set_context(M68kCpu* cpu, const void* src);
docs.text:
Restores context from src, while preserving destination-instance runtime bindings:

  • memory pointer and memory size
  • internal fault trap storage
  • installed callbacks

docs.title: bool m68k_load_srec(M68kCpu* cpu, const char* filename);
docs.text: Loads Motorola S-record data into memory. Returns false only when the file cannot be opened. Malformed records are reported to stderr and skipped. Entry-point records (S7/S8/S9) set cpu->pc directly.

docs.title: bool m68k_load_bin(M68kCpu* cpu, const char* filename, u32 address);
docs.text: Loads raw binary bytes into memory starting at address. Returns false only when the file cannot be opened.

docs.title: int m68k_disasm(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 pc, char* buffer, int buf_size);
docs.text: Disassembles one instruction at pc into buffer. Returns number of bytes consumed by that instruction.

docs.title: ROCKET68_VERSION_STR
docs.text: Semantic version string in include/rocket68.h.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: These examples focus on common host-integration patterns. All snippets use only public headers.

docs.title: 1. Run a Small Program from Reset Vectors
docs.text:
This example initializes RAM, writes reset vectors, and runs until STOP.

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#include "rocket68.h"

int main(void) {
    const u32 mem_size = 1024 * 1024;
    u8* ram = calloc(mem_size, 1);
    if (!ram) return 1;

    M68kCpu cpu;
    m68k_init(&cpu, ram, mem_size);

    /* Reset vectors */
    m68k_write_32(&cpu, 0x00000000, 0x0000FF00); /* SSP */
    m68k_write_32(&cpu, 0x00000004, 0x00000100); /* PC  */

    /* Program: NOP; STOP #$2700 */
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000100, 0x4E71);
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000102, 0x4E72);
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000104, 0x2700);

    m68k_reset(&cpu);

    while (!cpu.stopped) {
        m68k_execute(&cpu, 32);
    }

    printf("Stopped at PC=0x%08X\n", m68k_get_pc(&cpu));

    free(ram);
    return 0;
}

docs.title: 2. Start from Host-Controlled State
docs.text:
Use this when your platform sets CPU state directly instead of using vector table boot.

M68kCpu cpu;
m68k_init(&cpu, ram, mem_size);

m68k_set_ar(&cpu, 7, 0x0000FF00); /* A7/SP */
m68k_set_sr(&cpu, 0x2700);         /* Supervisor mode */
m68k_set_pc(&cpu, 0x00001000);

for (int i = 0; i < 100 && !cpu.stopped; i++) {
    m68k_step(&cpu);
}

docs.title: 3. Interrupt Handling with int_ack
docs.text:
m68k_set_irq sets a pending level (0-7). The core checks interrupts on step boundaries.

#include "rocket68.h"

static int my_int_ack(M68kCpu* cpu, int level) {
    (void)cpu;
    (void)level;

    /* Return autovector (level 3 -> vector 27). */
    return M68K_INT_ACK_AUTOVECTOR;
}

void setup_interrupt_demo(M68kCpu* cpu) {
    m68k_set_int_ack_callback(cpu, my_int_ack);

    /* Raise level-3 interrupt. */
    m68k_set_irq(cpu, 3);

    /* Run some time; interrupt will be taken if mask allows it. */
    m68k_execute(cpu, 128);
}

docs.title: 4. Model Wait States with wait_bus
docs.text:
wait_bus is called on memory reads, memory writes, and instruction fetches.

#include "rocket68.h"

static void wait_bus(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address, M68kSize size) {
    (void)address;
    (void)size;

    /* Add 2 extra cycles for each bus access. */
    cpu->cycles_remaining -= 2;
}

void attach_wait_states(M68kCpu* cpu) {
    m68k_set_wait_bus_callback(cpu, wait_bus);
}

docs.title: 5. Track Access Context with fc_callback
docs.text:
Function-code callback reports whether access is user/supervisor and data/program.

#include <stdio.h>
#include "rocket68.h"

static void fc_trace(M68kCpu* cpu, unsigned int fc) {
    (void)cpu;

    const char* name = "UNKNOWN";
    if (fc == M68K_FC_USER_DATA) name = "USER_DATA";
    else if (fc == M68K_FC_USER_PROG) name = "USER_PROG";
    else if (fc == M68K_FC_SUPV_DATA) name = "SUPV_DATA";
    else if (fc == M68K_FC_SUPV_PROG) name = "SUPV_PROG";

    printf("FC: %s (%u)\n", name, fc);
}

void attach_fc_trace(M68kCpu* cpu) {
    m68k_set_fc_callback(cpu, fc_trace);
}

docs.title: 6. Save and Restore CPU Context
docs.text:
Use this for save states or rewind.

#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include "rocket68.h"

void save_and_restore(M68kCpu* cpu) {
    const unsigned int ctx_size = m68k_context_size();
    void* blob = malloc(ctx_size);
    if (!blob) return;

    m68k_get_context(cpu, blob);

    /* Run forward a bit. */
    m68k_execute(cpu, 256);

    /* Restore prior state. */
    m68k_set_context(cpu, blob);

    free(blob);
}

Note:

  • m68k_set_context preserves the destination CPU's memory binding and installed callbacks.
  • Save-state data should be reused with the same Rocket68 build/config.

docs.title: 7. Load Programs from S-Record or Binary
docs.text:
#include "rocket68.h"

bool load_program(M68kCpu* cpu) {
    if (!m68k_load_srec(cpu, "firmware.srec")) {
        return false; /* File open failed. */
    }

    if (!m68k_load_bin(cpu, "overlay.bin", 0x00020000)) {
        return false; /* File open failed. */
    }

    return true;
}

Notes:

  • For loader edge-case behavior (malformed records, checksum policy, entry-point handling), see API Reference and Compatibility Notes.

docs.title: 8. Disassemble Memory for Debug Output
docs.text:
#include <stdio.h>
#include "rocket68.h"

void dump_disasm(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 pc, int count) {
    for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
        char text[128];
        int used = m68k_disasm(cpu, pc, text, (int)sizeof(text));

        printf("%08X: %s\n", pc, text);

        if (used <= 0) {
            break;
        }
        pc += (u32)used;
    }
}

For disassembly return semantics, see API Reference.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This page shows the smallest path from source files to a running 68000 program.

docs.title: Option A: Compile core sources directly
docs.text: gcc -std=c11 -O3 -Iinclude src/m68k/*.c your_app.c -o your_app

docs.title: Option B: Build library with Make
docs.text:
BUILD_TYPE=release make static
# Produces lib/librocket68.a

Then link your app:

gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Iinclude your_app.c lib/librocket68.a -o your_app

docs.title: 2. Minimal Runnable Program
docs.text:
This example:

  1. Creates CPU + RAM
  2. Writes reset vectors (SSP at 0x00000000, PC at 0x00000004)
  3. Writes a tiny program (NOP, then STOP #$2700)
  4. Runs until STOP
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

#include "m68k.h"

int main(void) {
    const u32 mem_size = 1024 * 1024;
    u8* ram = calloc(mem_size, 1);
    if (!ram) return 1;

    M68kCpu cpu;
    m68k_init(&cpu, ram, mem_size);

    /* Reset vectors */
    m68k_write_32(&cpu, 0x00000000, 0x0000FF00); /* initial SSP */
    m68k_write_32(&cpu, 0x00000004, 0x00000100); /* initial PC  */

    /* Program at 0x100: NOP; STOP #$2700 */
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000100, 0x4E71);
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000102, 0x4E72);
    m68k_write_16(&cpu, 0x00000104, 0x2700);

    m68k_reset(&cpu);

    int total_cycles = 0;
    while (!cpu.stopped) {
        total_cycles += m68k_execute(&cpu, 32);
    }

    printf("Stopped. cycles=%d pc=0x%08X\n", total_cycles, cpu.pc);

    free(ram);
    return 0;
}

docs.title: 3. Running Without Reset Vectors
docs.text:
You can also set state directly:

m68k_set_ar(&cpu, 7, 0x0000FF00); /* A7/SP */
m68k_set_sr(&cpu, 0x2700);         /* supervisor mode */
m68k_set_pc(&cpu, 0x00000100);

Use this pattern when your host controls boot flow explicitly.

docs.title: 4. Basic Execution Model
docs.text:
• m68k_step(&cpu) executes one instruction (with interrupt/trace checks).
  • m68k_execute(&cpu, cycles) runs until the timeslice is exhausted.
  • m68k_cycles_run(&cpu) and m68k_cycles_remaining(&cpu) report timeslice accounting.
  • m68k_end_timeslice(&cpu) forces the current timeslice to finish.

docs.title: 5. Callback Wiring (Optional)
docs.text:
static void wait_bus(M68kCpu* cpu, u32 address, M68kSize size) {
    (void)address;
    (void)size;
    /* Add 2 wait-state cycles for each access */
    cpu->cycles_remaining -= 2;
}

static int int_ack(M68kCpu* cpu, int level) {
    (void)cpu;
    (void)level;
    return M68K_INT_ACK_AUTOVECTOR;
}

m68k_set_wait_bus_callback(&cpu, wait_bus);
m68k_set_int_ack_callback(&cpu, int_ack);

docs.title: 6. Useful Notes
docs.text:
• Memory accesses are checked against cpu.memory_size; out-of-range accesses trigger bus error handling.
  • Internally, addresses are masked to 24-bit before validation.
  • M68kCpu state is instance-local, so multiple CPU instances can run independently.

docs.title: Compatibility Notes
docs.text: This page lists current compatibility notes and scope limits based on the current codebase.

docs.title: CPU Model Scope
docs.text:
• The core exposes one execution profile through M68kCpu.
  • There is no public API to select CPU model variants (for example 68010/68020 mode switches).
  • Some later-family instructions exist (MOVEC, MOVES, RTD, BKPT), but model behavior is not fully parameterized.

docs.title: Address Space and Memory Model
docs.text:
• All memory accesses are against one flat memory buffer (cpu->memory, cpu->memory_size).
  • Addresses are masked to 24-bit (address & 0x00FFFFFF) before bounds checks.
  • There is no per-access memory read/write callback API for custom bus mapping; integration is currently done by sharing a memory buffer plus optional timing callbacks.

docs.title: Callback Behavior Notes
docs.text:
• fc_callback is emitted for memory reads/writes and instruction fetches.
  • M68K_FC_INT_ACK is defined, but the current interrupt acknowledge path does not emit FC callback events with this code.
  • pc_changed_callback is triggered when PC is changed through m68k_set_pc.
  • Direct PC writes (for example in m68k_reset, m68k_fetch, and S-record entry-point load) do not call pc_changed_callback.
  • reset_callback is tied to execution of the RESET instruction, not to m68k_reset().
  • illg_callback can be installed, but the current decode/exception path does not call it.

docs.title: Control Registers and Exception Base
docs.text:
• VBR, SFC, and DFC fields exist and are accessible through MOVEC.
  • Exception vector fetch currently uses vector * 4 from base address zero.
  • VBR is not currently applied as an exception vector base in m68k_exception.
  • SFC/DFC values are stored but not used to drive bus access behavior.

docs.title: Context Save/Restore Format
docs.text:
• m68k_get_context / m68k_set_context copy raw M68kCpu struct bytes.
  • The blob format should be treated as build-dependent (compiler/ABI/version sensitive), not a stable cross-version interchange format.
  • m68k_set_context preserves destination instance memory binding and installed callbacks.

docs.title: Loader and Disassembler Notes
docs.text:
• m68k_load_srec and m68k_load_bin return false only when file open fails.
  • m68k_load_srec reports malformed lines and continues parsing.
  • S-record checksum validity is not explicitly validated.
  • S-record entry records (S7/S8/S9) set cpu->pc directly.
  • m68k_disasm returns instruction bytes consumed; unsupported decode cases may still produce ??? output text.

docs.title: JSON Compatibility Harness
docs.text:
• The JSON compatibility runner (tests/test_json.c) has a relaxed default for exception-path state checks.
  • Strict exception-path checking is available with ROCKET68_JSON_STRICT=1.
  • This behavior is test-harness policy, not a runtime core API toggle.
